phosra_to_youtube.all_ages: YouTube Kids: Preschool or Younger (or Approved Content Only for strictest control)
phosra_to_youtube.ages_7_plus: YouTube Kids: Older or Supervised: Explore
phosra_to_youtube.ages_13_plus: Supervised: Explore More + Restricted Mode ON
phosra_to_youtube.adults_only: Supervised: Most of YouTube or unrestricted YouTube

Preschool.description: Content for youngest viewers (ages 4 and under). Designed to promote creativity, playfulness, learning, and exploration.

Younger.description: Wider variety for early elementary (ages 5-8). Meets widening interests of younger kids. Includes all Preschool content plus expanded categories.

Older.description: More grown-up filtered content (ages 9-12). For older kids ready for content excluded from Younger and Preschool. Includes all Younger and Preschool content.

Approved Content Only.description: Parent manually approves every collection, channel, and video. Most restrictive option. Only hand-picked content is available to the child.

Explore.description: Content generally suitable for ages 9+. Vlogs, tutorials, gaming, music, news, educational content, DIY, arts and crafts, dance. Does not include live streams.

Explore More.description: Broader range for ages 13+. Everything in Explore plus live streams, content with some mature themes including sexual education topics, non-explicit accounts of sexual experiences, and educational videos on sexual and gender identity.

Most of YouTube.description: Nearly all YouTube content except videos marked as 18+ and other videos that may not be appropriate for supervised experiences. For older teens.

ON.description: Binary filter that hides potentially mature content using automated signals (video metadata, title, language, age restrictions) and human reviewers. Also hides all comments. Not 100% accurate.

dns_enforcement.how: Set DNS CNAME record for youtube.com and www.youtube.com to the appropriate hostname

OFF.description: No content filtering. All YouTube content visible including age-gated content (if account age is verified 18+). Comments, live chat, and all features available.

ai_age_estimation.method: Uses viewing patterns, account age, content preferences, and other signals to estimate user age regardless of stated birthday
ai_age_estimation.consequence: Users estimated under 18 get automatic teen protections: disabled personalized ads, content safeguards, default-on wellbeing tools
ai_age_estimation.appeal: Users incorrectly flagged can verify with government-issued ID (drivers license, state ID, passport)

age_gated_content.description: Videos deemed inappropriate for viewers under 18. Requires signed-in account with verified age. YouTube AI age estimation (2025) may automatically restrict access for estimated minors.

content_descriptors.notes: YouTube does not expose content descriptors (violence, language, nudity) as individually filterable categories. Content filtering is tier-based only (YouTube Kids levels, supervised account levels, or Restricted Mode on/off). YouTube uses automated classification, creator self-designation (Made for Kids), and community guidelines enforcement for content categorization.

safeSearch.description: YouTube Data API v3 search parameter. 'strict' excludes all restricted content from search results. 'moderate' is the default and filters some content. 'none' returns everything.

madeForKids.description: COPPA compliance flag. Set by creator or YouTube's automated system. Required since 2019 FTC settlement. Videos marked madeForKids disable personalized ads, comments, notifications, live chat, and more.

contentRating.description: Country-specific content ratings. Includes mpaatRating (MPAA for trailers), bbfcRating (UK), fskRating (Germany), and many others. Most user-generated content will NOT have these ratings.

key_properties.ytRating: YouTube's own age restriction: 'ytAgeRestricted' when video is age-gated to 18+
key_properties.mpaatRating: MPAA rating for movie trailers (mpaatG, mpaatPg, mpaatPg13, mpaatR, mpaatNc17)

MPAA.notes: Only available for professionally rated content (movie trailers, licensed films) via contentDetails.contentRating.mpaatRating. Most YouTube content (user-generated) has no MPAA rating.

TV_Parental_Guidelines.notes: Not applicable. YouTube content is not TV-rated. No TV ratings in the API.

Common_Sense_Media.notes: Common Sense Media independently rates some YouTube channels and content. YouTube does not integrate CSM ratings into its filtering. Phosra could cross-reference CSM data externally.

YouTube_Self_Rating.notes: YouTube's native content classification system. Uploaders must self-declare Made for Kids status (COPPA requirement since 2019). YouTube may override with automated classification.

Google_Family_Link_Levels.notes: Google Family Link provides tiered supervision for children's YouTube access. These are the parental control tiers, not content ratings per se.
